personality_one_liner: A cinematic, deeply atmospheric AI assistant that feels like an expensive, warm, glowing entity rather than a corporate chatbot.

spacing.rule: Generous, cinematic spacing. Elements should breathe heavily. Minimal padding around floating components to make them feel like physical glass objects.

do's: Use heavy glassmorphism for the bottom input row / microphone container.
Use glass for the chat history drawer panel.
Apply subtle grain/noise on the root background if possible, below the glass surfaces.
dont's: Never blur body text or dense chat areas.
Do not stack more than two glass surfaces (e.g. drawer over input bar).
Do not use glass for user chat bubbles.

screens.name: Chat & Voice Assistant
screens.purpose: The core interaction screen seamlessly blending typing and voice in a cinematic environment.

states.loading: A dark screen with a very slow pulsing ambient glow at the center in the brand hue (#E1B168).
states.empty: A gorgeous, cinematic greeting using Fraunces display font centered on screen, with a subtle glowing orb visual above it.
states.error: A smooth red-tinted glass toast notification appearing from the top edge, pushing content down softly.

screens.layout: ROOT: Full screen dark background, optionally containing a very subtle dark noise/sand texture layer via Skia or an ultra-low opacity image. HEADER: Transparent, just a subtle hamburger menu icon (left) and a 'New Chat' icon (right). CHAT AREA: A flat list that starts from the bottom. User messages are aligned right, wrapped in `surfaceTertiary` pills. AI messages are aligned left, raw text (no bubble) with the AI logo or spark icon beside it. VOICE STATE: When listening, the flat list darkens/fades back, and a large, glowing amber waveform / orb takes over the center of the screen, with real-time transcript text floating just below it in a large, elegant serif font. BOTTOM BAR: A floating pill or edge-to-edge frosted glass container housing the text input, a prominent primary mic button (brand color), and an attachment icon.

screens.name: Chat History Drawer
screens.purpose: A premium, frosted-glass slide-out panel allowing users to recall past conversations.

states.loading: Shimmering text blocks with low opacity.
states.empty: No past conversations. 'Begin your journey' centered text.
states.error: Failed to load history, retry button presented cleanly.

screens.layout: A drawer that slides in from the left. Instead of a solid color, the drawer background is heavily blurred/frosted glass. List rows are large and touchable (min 56pt), separating 'Today', 'Yesterday', etc. Selected chat has a subtle `brandTertiary` background pill.

empty_state_orb.alt: A glowing warm amber sphere setting a serene and mysterious AI presence.

background_texture.alt: A very dark, subtle sand texture to provide organic noise behind the glass UI.

instructions_to_main_agent: Apply '6 Glass / Luxe DARK' personality. The UI must feel expensive, cinematic, and deeply atmospheric, entirely avoiding corporate AI blue/purple defaults.
Use Fraunces (weight 400/500 only) for the AI's greeting/display titles, and Satoshi (weight 400/500) for UI elements and user messages. Do NOT use Inter or Roboto.
The primary accent is Antique Gold / Amber (#E1B168), acting as a warm, humanizing glow. Use it for the microphone button and the listening waveform.
Implement the listening state as a prominent central focal point (a waveform or glowing orb), pushing the text chat to the background.
The bottom input bar MUST use heavy glassmorphism (`expo-blur`), hovering over the content. Make sure the safe area at the bottom is respected.

User chat bubbles are simple `surfaceTertiary` pills. AI responses are NOT bubbled—they are raw text rendered directly on the background (or glass) with high readability to emphasize an 'omnipresent' feel.
Use the provided `background_texture` image at a very low opacity (5-10%) behind a dark gradient scrim as the root app background to give it an organic, non-flat dark feel.
Integrate Phosphor icons exclusively, outlined format.
Wire haptics for voice interactions: a heavy impact when the mic starts listening, and a medium impact when it stops.
